---
title: "A question in plain English?"
description: "One line: the method, the data, the scope."
date: 2026-01-01
categories: [R, tool-two, topic]
image: "../assets/figures/your-thumbnail.png"
image-alt: "Short description of the thumbnail figure."
---

<!--
COPY THIS FILE to projects/<slug>.qmd for each new project. Do not edit this
template in place - the leading underscore keeps it off the site.

Rules:
- Title is a plain-English question, not a method name.
- categories become the filter pills and per-card badges: use real tool names.
- Keep the body roughly 600-900 words.
- Follow the section order below. The "Assumptions and limitations" section is
  required, not optional.
- Captions state the finding, not the chart type.
- Use hyphens, never em dashes.
- One language per page (knitr for R, jupyter for Python). Do not mix.
-->

## The question

One or two sentences, non-technical. State what you were trying to find out and
why it matters. A reviewer should understand the point before hitting any code.

## Data and cleaning

Source, size, granularity, and time span. Then the messy parts you resolved -
composite keys, mixed formats, joins that needed safeguarding. The cleaning is
often more impressive than the model, so do not skip it.

[View the code and data on GitHub](https://github.com/yourusername/your-repo)

## Method, and why this one

Name the method, then justify the choice against the obvious alternative. Not
just "I used fixed effects" but why fixed effects rather than pooled OLS.

> A one-line pull-quote carrying the key methodological insight goes here. This
> is the sentence you most want a reviewer to actually read.

## Findings

Display one or two figures. Preferred pattern is a pre-exported PNG:

::: {.column-page}
![Fig 1. Caption states the finding, not the chart type.](../assets/figures/your-figure.png){.figure-wide}
:::

State the headline numbers in the prose around the figure.

## Assumptions and limitations

Required section. List the assumptions the method rests on (for example:
independence, normality, no time-varying confounders, patient-level splitting to
prevent leakage) and say which you checked and which you could not. Then state
what the result does not license you to claim.

## Reproducing this

Brief note on how someone would run it: the repo, the lockfile, and whether the
data is public.
